summary: Implemented Phase 4 resource policy hardening. Added a fixed Steel/Oil `ResourceCost` helper in rules, PlayerState cost wrappers, faction-catalog ability legality, and command-path checks so out-of-faction build/train/research/ability attempts do not spend Steel/Oil or reserve Supply. Documented Steel/Oil/Supply as the intentional faction economy contract, added approved direct-resource module notes, updated lifecycle notes, and marked `plans/faction/phase-4.md` done.
verification: rustfmt on touched Rust files: passed
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rts-cargo-target/faction-phase-4 cargo test --manifest-path server/Cargo.toml -p rts-sim fixture_faction: passed
gameplay_impact: No intended current-faction gameplay change. Kriegsia still uses the same Steel/Oil/Supply resources, costs, supply reservation, HUD/resource payloads, command-card costs, score values, replay analysis values, and prediction policy. Future factions are now more explicitly constrained to the same Steel/Oil/Supply economy until a separate generic-resource migration exists.
next_executor_notes: Phase 5 can define faction starting Steel/Oil/Supply and supply rules in loadout data without changing resource payload shape. Use `rules::economy::ResourceCost`, `resource_cost`, `resource_shortage_notice_for_cost`, and `PlayerState::{can_afford,spend_cost,refund_cost}` for catalog-sourced costs. Approved direct Steel/Oil/Supply modules are documented in `docs/design/balance.md`; `scripts/check-faction-assumptions.mjs` now checks that resource policy note exists. No commit, merge, push, PR, or final review pass was performed.
manual_test_notes: Later manual sanity should start a normal Kriegsia match and verify Steel/Oil/Supply HUD rows, gathering, building, training, research, Artillery point-fire spending, spectator resource rows, score screen values, and replay-analysis values still look correct.
